title: Anyone else had an AI trading bot execute a stupid trade that cost real money? Built BotShield to stop it before execution.
author: Yeahbudz_
selftext:
I've been running automated trading agents and seen the same nightmare too many times — the model hallucinates a bad order, creeps outside risk limits, or just does something dumb right before it hits the exchange.

I built **BotShield** to fix exactly that.

It sits right before your bot sends any order and does three simple things:

* Checks against your hard boundaries (size, assets, leverage rules, time-of-day limits, etc.)
* Returns SAFE / WARNING / BLOCK + a quick risk score
* Gives you a signed, tamper-proof receipt for every decision so you have real proof if something goes wrong

Early private beta is $499/month with personal setup help. Only a few spots left this month.

Has anyone here dealt with model hallucinations or scope creep in live bots? Would a pre-execution guard like this be useful, or am I missing the real pain point?

Landing page + demo: [https://commonguy25.github.io/botshield-landing](https://commonguy25.github.io/botshield-landing)
